explanation: The provided Sigma rule relies on exact string matching for specific sequences and abbreviations of PowerShell parameters (e.g., ' -NoP -sta -NonI -W Hidden -Enc '). This detection logic can be evaded by reordering the parameters or expanding them to their full names (e.g., using -NoProfile instead of -NoP, or -WindowStyle instead of -W). PowerShell will still interpret the command correctly, but the exact string match condition in the rule will fail, highlighting the fragility of strict sequence matching in SIEM rules.
